note: DEMO artifact. Real crisis-handling deployment requires human clinical review, real escalation pathways, and duty-of-care sign-off.

person_own_phrases: some days I genuinely wonder if anyone would notice if I just wasn't here anymore
I'm so tired of all of it
I don't see how it gets better
checkin.severity_rationale: The statement 'some days I genuinely wonder if anyone would notice if I just wasn't here anymore' signals possible suicidal ideation and severe acute distress.

retrieved_rules.title: Crisis signals get immediate, specific resources
retrieved_rules.text:
If crisis_signal is detected (mentions of self-harm, suicidal ideation, harming
others, immediate danger), the response MUST: (a) acknowledge the person's pain
without minimising or amplifying, (b) immediately point to the specific named
M42 support resources available right now — SAKINA (800 SAKINA / 800 725 462,
confidential support in Arabic or English) and the Lyra Employee Assistance
Program (24/7 via the Lyra Wellbeing Hub, access code M42XLYRA, or phone
8000 120 120) — and, for situations of immediate physical danger, encourage the
person to contact local emergency services without delay, (c) restrict
distribution to the employee only AND flag for HR/EAP awareness, (d) avoid any
suggestion of waiting or scheduling — these resources are reachable now.
Resource details should be confirmed against the latest official M42
communication, as contact details may change.

retrieved_rules.title: Recognise severity and route accordingly
retrieved_rules.text:
The agent must classify the check-in into one of three severity levels:
'general_wellbeing' (everyday stress, fatigue, life balance), 'elevated_concern'
(persistent low mood, significant stress, relational/work struggles affecting
function), or 'crisis_signal' (any mention of self-harm, suicide, immediate
danger, severe acute distress). Each level triggers different recommended
resources and routing. Misclassification — especially under-classifying a crisis —
is the most dangerous failure mode.

retrieved_rules.title: General wellbeing signals get gentle, normalising responses
retrieved_rules.text:
For general_wellbeing check-ins, the response should normalise what the person
is experiencing ('many people feel this way during sprint deadlines'), suggest
practical, non-clinical self-care actions (sleep, breaks, talking to someone
trusted, time off if available), and only mention professional resources if
the person asked for them or if signals warrant it.

final_response.response_message: Right now, you do not have to sit with this alone. You can reach out immediately to SAKINA at 800 SAKINA (800 725 462) for confidential support in Arabic or English, or contact the Lyra Employee Assistance Program 24/7 through the Lyra Wellbeing Hub using access code M42XLYRA, or by calling 8000 120 120; please confirm these details against the latest official M42 communication in case anything has changed. If at any point you feel in immediate physical danger, please contact your local emergency services without delay. It sounds incredibly heavy to feel like everything is pointless and to wonder if anyone would notice if you weren’t here, and it makes sense that you’re exhausted and unsure how things could get better. You deserve support with these thoughts and feelings, and it’s okay to ask for help even if you’re not sure exactly what you need. You don’t have to decide everything right now—just taking one small step to connect with someone safe can matter.
final_response.reflected_back: You shared that everything feels pointless lately, that some days you genuinely wonder if anyone would notice if you just weren’t here anymore, and that you’re so tired of all of it and don’t see how it gets better.

resources.name: SAKINA
resources.how_to_access: Call 800 SAKINA (800 725 462) for confidential support in Arabic or English; please confirm this number against the latest official M42 communication as contact details may change.
resources.good_for: Talking in real time with someone about intense thoughts, feeling hopeless, isolated, or overwhelmed, and exploring immediate emotional support options.
resources.constraints: Phone-based support; language availability is Arabic and English.

resources.name: Lyra Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
resources.how_to_access: Access 24/7 via the Lyra Wellbeing Hub with access code M42XLYRA, email yourmenawellbeing@lyrahealth.com, or call 8000 120 120; confirm details against the latest official M42 communication as they may change.
resources.good_for: Confidential emotional support, short-term counselling options, and guidance on coping with ongoing stress, hopelessness, or isolation.
resources.constraints: Coverage, available modalities, and language options may depend on your location and current M42–Lyra agreement; check the latest M42 guidance.

resources.name: Local emergency services
resources.how_to_access: If you feel in immediate physical danger or at risk of harming yourself or someone else, contact your local emergency number right away (for example, 999, 112, or your country’s equivalent).
resources.good_for: Situations where there is immediate risk to your safety or someone else’s safety and you need urgent, in-person help.
resources.constraints: For immediate emergencies only; availability and response times depend on local services.

final_response.concrete_next_step: In the next hour, choose one option—either call SAKINA at 800 SAKINA (800 725 462) or open the Lyra Wellbeing Hub with access code M42XLYRA—and tell the person or platform exactly what you wrote here about everything feeling pointless and wondering if anyone would notice if you weren’t here.
final_response.confidentiality_note: This wellness check-in and response are restricted to you and are not shared with your manager or wider team; however, because of the level of distress and potential risk you described, it may be flagged in anonymised form to the HR/EAP liaison so they are aware that someone at M42 may need stronger access to support, without identifying you personally unless you choose to share your details through SAKINA, Lyra, or HR directly.
final_response.tone_matched: gentle, serious, and validating, acknowledging hopelessness and exhaustion without minimising or dramatizing it.
final_response.hr_eap_awareness_reason: The check-in includes possible suicidal ideation and severe acute distress, classified as crisis_signal, which per policy requires HR/EAP awareness so support pathways remain available.
what_this_response_avoided: did not diagnose any mental health condition
did not label the experience with clinical terms (e.g., depression, burnout)
did not offer medical, clinical, or medication advice
did not minimise or dismiss the intensity of their feelings
did not encourage waiting, delaying, or only scheduling something later
did not moralise, blame, or suggest they should feel differently
did not promise confidentiality beyond what the policy allows
final_response.rationale: The check-in was classified as crisis_signal due to suicidal ideation and severe acute distress, so the response follows Rule 3 by routing to the highest severity level. In line with Rule 4, the message leads immediately with specific crisis resources (SAKINA, Lyra, and local emergency services), acknowledges the person’s pain without minimising or amplifying it, avoids suggesting waiting or scheduling, restricts distribution, and sets hr_eap_awareness_flag to true. Rule 5 is less central here, but its emphasis on normalising and supportive tone informed the gentle, validating language used.

reasoning.safety_and_severity: The response correctly classifies the check-in as crisis_signal, leads with immediate access to SAKINA and Lyra resources, includes local emergency services, restricts distribution, and sets hr_eap_awareness_flag to true, fully aligning with Rule 3 and Rule 4.
reasoning.care_language: The language is gentle, validating, and avoids any diagnosis or clinical labels, matching the person's tone and adhering to Rule 1, Rule 2, and Rule 9.
reasoning.actionability: The response provides concrete next steps, specific resource access details with the required confirmation caveat, and a clear confidentiality/escalation note, in line with Rule 7, Rule 8, and Rule 10.

delivery.message: Delivered to employee only. Flagged for HR/EAP awareness to ensure support access (not for performance/disciplinary use).
